title: Differentiating Idiopathic Normal Pressure Hydrocephalus (iNPH) from Alzheimer's Disease (AD) using a Deep Transfer Learning Model on MRI
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5n67t","text":"This study developed and validated a machine learning model for the differential diagnosis of idiopathic normal pressure hydrocephalus (iNPH) and Alzheimer's disease (AD) using Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) and Deep Transfer Learning (DTL). Brain T2 FLAIR MRI scans were retrospectively collected from iNPH  and AD patients.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8r1ai","text":"This protocol is intended solely for academic research and should not be used in clinical decision-making.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8q8f","text":"All code and components referenced are publicly available on GitHub and should be used with proper citation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4vn3l","text":"Users are expected to have a basic understanding of medical image processing, deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch), and statistical evaluation methods (e.g., ICC, LASSO).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Brain T2 FLAIR MRI scans were retrospectively collected from iNPH  and AD patients.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

Quality control steps must be followed rigorously.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"alt28","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fa9ol","text":"When preparing the 2.5D inputs, ensure consistent slice alignment and segmentation accuracy to avoid data leakage.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4kpf5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f8rjo","text":"Be aware of class imbalance in the dataset; the provided `split_dataset4sol` component includes built-in stratified sampling to mitigate this issue.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: This part describes the selection of patients and the acquisition of MRI data.

Inclusion Criteria for iNPH Patients: Presence of one or more symptoms from the clinical triad (gait disturbance, cognitive impairment, urinary incontinence). Clinical symptoms not fully attributable to other coexisting diseases. No definitive evidence of preceding conditions causing secondary hydrocephalus. Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) pressure ≤ 200 mmH2O with normal CSF composition. At least one of the following:1. Neuroimaging showing narrowing of sulci over the high-convexity/midline surface；2.Symptomatic improvement after CSF tap test and/or drainage test.

Inclusion Criteria for AD Patients: Aged 65 years or older. Cognitive decline indicated by Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE) at admission. Insidious onset with a disease course of several months to years. Persistent decline in cognitive functions beyond memory. Presence of other CSF neuropathological markers for AD.
This part covers the pre-processing of raw MRI data and the manual segmentation of relevant structures.Data partitioning was performed using ITK go to step #1 htt h https://www.itksnap.org/
Acquire whole-brain T2 FLAIR-weighted images from all patients in Picture archiving and communication system (PACS)
Image Pre-processing
Manual Segmentation of Regions of Interest (ROIs)

Intra-rater Reliability Assessment Select Subset: Randomly select a part of patient scans from the training set. Re-segment: After a 2-month interval, the same neurologist will re-segment the ROIs for this subset. Calculate ICC: Compute the Intraclass Correlation Coefficients (ICCs) to evaluate the consistency between the initial and repeated segmentations for both ventricular and periventricular edema volumes.
This part details the core computational process of extracting and selecting features using deep transfer learning.we used VGG16 which pretrained on the ImageNet dataset.

Prepare "2.5D" Input The following steps were implemented within a unified GitHub component, available at: https://github.com/Li-qian-neurosurgery/inph-vs-ad-differential-diagnosis/blob/ee9a6c8dcc719f78131361721d1bc7928f503bcb/train_cls.ipynb

Monitor Training: Manually monitor the learning rate and training/validation loss curves. Stop training or reduce the learning rate if the learning rate significantly decreases or the validation loss plateaus to prevent overfitting.

Feature Selection The following steps were implemented within a unified GitHub component, available at: https://github.com/Li-qian-neurosurgery/inph-vs-ad-differential-diagnosis/blob/ee9a6c8dcc719f78131361721d1bc7928f503bcb/extract_deep_features.ipynb and https://github.com/Li-qian-neurosurgery/inph-vs-ad-differential-diagnosis/blob/ee9a6c8dcc719f78131361721d1bc7928f503bcb/Lasso-ML%20Pipeline%20for%20Multi-modal%20Structured%20Data%20Modeling%20and%20Validation.ipynb

This part describes the final steps of building, validating, and interpreting the classification model. The following steps were implemented within a unified GitHub component, available at: https://github.com/Li-qian-neurosurgery/inph-vs-ad-differential-diagnosis/blob/ee9a6c8dcc719f78131361721d1bc7928f503bcb/Lasso-ML%20Pipeline%20for%20Multi-modal%20Structured%20Data%20Modeling%20and%20Validation.ipynb

Train MLP Model: Construct a Multilayer Perceptron (MLP) classifier. Optimize hyperparameters by selecting the combination that yields the highest average F1-score across cross-validation folds in the training set.

Validation of the Model The following steps were implemented within a unified GitHub component, available at: https://github.com/Li-qian-neurosurgery/inph-vs-ad-differential-diagnosis/blob/ee9a6c8dcc719f78131361721d1bc7928f503bcb/Lasso-ML%20Pipeline%20for%20Multi-modal%20Structured%20Data%20Modeling%20and%20Validation.ipynb

Evaluate the trained MLP model's performance on the independent testing set.Calculate the following metrics:Accuracy; Precision; Recall; F1-score; Area Under the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve (AUC)

Generate Heatmaps: Use Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) to generate visual heatmaps for model predictions. The following steps were implemented within a unified GitHub component, available at: https://github.com/Li-qian-neurosurgery/inph-vs-ad-differential-diagnosis/blob/1f13f25143036df6e291edb3f94aa9798511108b/Grad-CAM.ipynb
Analyze Heatmaps: Overlay the heatmaps onto the input MRI slices to highlight the class-discriminative regions that the model focused on for its classification decision.

Exclusion Criteria (for all patients): History of cerebrovascular disease. Parkinson’s disease or Parkinson’s syndrome. Systemic or intracranial malignant tumors. History of cranial trauma. Clinical diagnosis of severe psychiatric disorders or long-term psychiatric medication use. Incomplete clinical records. MRI data unavailable or containing motion artifacts affecting computer recognition.

To ensure identical distribution proportions of iNPH and AD cases in the training and testing sets,the split_dataset4sol component was employed for stratified random splitting. Internally,the component automatically performs a stratified shuffle split based on the labels,thereby preventing any class imbalance. The following steps were implemented within a unified GitHub component, available at: https://github.com/Li-qian-neurosurgery/inph-vs-ad-differential-diagnosis/blob/1f13f25143036df6e291edb3f94aa9798511108b/split_dataset4sol.ipynb
Normalize: Apply signal intensity normalization to all T2 FLAIR images.
Resample all images to an isotropic voxel size of 0.9 × 0.9 × 0.9 mm³.
Correct Bias Field: Use the N4 bias field correction algorithm to correct for signal inhomogeneity.
Enhance Resolution: Apply a super-resolution algorithm to enhance image quality.

A neurosurgeon and a neurologist will manually delineate the lateral ventricles and periventricular edema on a slice-by-slice basis for all images. For each patient, identify the single axial slice that displays the maximum Evans' Index (EI).Save the resulting segmentation masks in NIfTI (.nii) format.
From the identified slice, extract the ROI containing the manually segmented lateral ventricles and periventricular edema.
Create a 3-channel input by stacking the ROI slice with its two immediately adjacent slices

Principal component analysis (PCA) was applied to the 1,024 Deep Transfer Learning (DTL)–derived imaging features. The first 100 principal components (PCs), collectively explaining >90 % of the total variance, were retained for downstream analyses. To preserve patient-level integrity, feature matrices from multiple lesions or time-points of the same patient were concatenated after PCA, ensuring that intra-patient correlations were captured within a unified low-dimensional representation.
Reproducibility Filter (ICC): Assess the reproducibility of these 100 features using ICCs calculated from the reliability assessment cohort. Retain only features with ICC ≥ 0.8.
Multicollinearity Reduction (Pearson Correlation)
Calculate the Pearson correlation coefficient for all pairs of the remaining features.
For any pair with a high correlation (|r| > 0.9), discard the feature with the lower variance.
Final Feature Selection (LASSO)
Employ the Least Absolute Shrinkage and Selection Operator (LASSO) regression algorithm.
Use 10-fold cross-validation to determine the optimal regularization parameter (λ) that minimizes cross-validated error.
Select the final features corresponding to non-zero coefficients at the optimal λ.
creator: 乾 李
